data.title: dMY Squared Secures $110M PIPE Financing Deal
data.description:
TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

dMY Squared Technology Group, Inc. Class A ( (DMYY) ) has shared an announcement.

On December 4, 2025, dMY Squared Technology Group, Inc., Rose Holdco Pte. Ltd., and Horizon Quantum Computing Pte. Ltd. entered into PIPE Subscription Agreements with investors for a $110 million PIPE financing to support their proposed business combination. The financing, which exceeded Horizon Quantum’s original target by over 120%, includes investments from IonQ and other strategic investors. This capital will be used to accelerate Horizon Quantum’s R&D and advance its quantum computing infrastructure, positioning the company to capitalize on the growing quantum computing market.

More about dMY Squared Technology Group, Inc. Class A

dMY Squared Technology Group, Inc. is a publicly traded special purpose acquisition company focused on mergers, capital stock exchanges, asset acquisitions, stock purchases, reorganizations, or similar business combinations. Horizon Quantum Computing Pte. Ltd. is a pioneer in software infrastructure for quantum applications, aiming to bridge the gap between current hardware and future applications through advanced quantum software development tools.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $54.3M

data.title: Kroger’s Q3 2025: Mixed Results Amid Strategic Shifts
data.description:
Kroger Company ( (KR) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Kroger Company presented to its investors.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Kroger Company, a leading American retail company, operates a network of supermarkets and eCommerce platforms, serving millions of customers daily with a focus on food and grocery retailing.

In its third quarter of 2025, Kroger reported a mixed financial performance with significant strategic developments. The company highlighted a notable increase in eCommerce sales and completed a strategic review aimed at making its eCommerce business profitable by 2026.

Key financial metrics for the quarter included a 2.6% increase in identical sales excluding fuel and a 17% rise in eCommerce sales. Despite these gains, the company reported an operating loss of $1,541 million, primarily due to a $2.6 billion impairment charge related to its automated fulfillment network. Adjusted FIFO operating profit stood at $1,089 million, with an adjusted EPS of $1.05.

Kroger’s strategic initiatives included an accelerated share repurchase program and a focus on improving customer experience and operational efficiency. The company also made a significant contribution to multi-employer pension plans, impacting its operating expenses.

Looking ahead, Kroger remains committed to driving long-term growth through strategic investments and maintaining its financial health. The company has updated its full-year guidance, narrowing its identical sales growth forecast and slightly raising its adjusted EPS outlook for 2025.

data.title: SAIC Reports Q3 FY2026 Results and Raises Guidance
data.description:
Science Applications International Corp. ( (SAIC) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Science Applications International Corp. presented to its investors.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Science Applications International Corporation (SAIC) is a Fortune 500 technology integrator that provides mission-critical solutions across defense, space, civilian, and intelligence markets, focusing on digital transformation and innovation.

In its third quarter of fiscal year 2026, SAIC reported revenues of $1.87 billion, with net bookings of $2.2 billion and a book-to-bill ratio of 1.2. The company achieved a net income of $78 million and an adjusted EBITDA of $185 million, representing 9.9% of revenues.

Key financial metrics showed a decrease in revenues by 6% compared to the previous year, primarily due to contract completions and the impact of a government shutdown. Despite this, the company maintained strong margins and increased its fiscal year 2026 guidance. Notable contract awards included a $1.4 billion task order from the U.S. Air Force and a $413 million task order from the U.S. Army, among others.

SAIC’s strategic focus remains on long-term shareholder value creation, with initiatives to fuel growth and enhance margins. The company’s backlog at the end of the quarter was approximately $23.8 billion, indicating a robust pipeline of future work.

Looking ahead, SAIC has increased its fiscal year 2026 guidance, expecting revenues between $7.275 billion and $7.325 billion, with an adjusted EBITDA of approximately $695 million. The company aims to continue its trajectory of growth and innovation in the technology integration sector.

data.title: Smith & Wesson Reports Q2 Fiscal 2026 Results
data.description:
Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. ( (SWBI) ) has released its Q2 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. presented to its investors.

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. is a prominent U.S.-based company specializing in the manufacturing and design of firearms, including handguns, long guns, and suppressors, catering to both consumer and professional markets globally. The company also offers manufacturing services and firearm training programs.

In its latest earnings report for the second quarter of fiscal 2026, Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. reported net sales of $124.7 million, a slight decrease from the previous year. Despite the drop in sales, the company maintained a gross margin of 24.3% and reported a net income of $1.9 million, translating to earnings per share of $0.04.

Key financial highlights include a decrease in net sales by 3.9% compared to the same quarter last year, and a reduction in gross margin from 26.6% to 24.3%. The company also reported a significant increase in net cash provided by operating activities, reaching $27.3 million, which marks an improvement of $34.7 million from the previous year. Additionally, new products accounted for 38.7% of sales, showcasing the company’s focus on innovation.

Looking ahead, Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. remains optimistic despite macroeconomic uncertainties. The company anticipates an 8-10% increase in third-quarter sales compared to the previous year, driven by strong brand performance and new product offerings. The board has also authorized a quarterly dividend, reflecting confidence in the company’s financial health.

data.title: Ulta Beauty Reports Strong Q3 2025 Results
data.description:
Ulta Beauty ( (ULTA) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Ulta Beauty presented to its investors.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Ulta Beauty, Inc. is the largest specialty beauty retailer in the U.S., offering a wide range of cosmetics, skincare, haircare, and salon services, with a significant presence in both physical stores and online platforms.

In its third quarter fiscal 2025 earnings report, Ulta Beauty announced a 12.9% increase in net sales, reaching $2.9 billion, compared to the same period last year. The company also reported a comparable sales increase of 6.3%, alongside a net income of $230.9 million, or $5.14 per diluted share. Ulta Beauty has revised its fiscal 2025 guidance upwards, reflecting strong performance and strategic growth initiatives.

Key financial highlights include a gross profit increase to 40.4% of net sales, driven by lower inventory shrink and higher merchandise margins. However, selling, general, and administrative expenses rose to 29.4% of net sales due to higher incentive compensation and store-related costs. The company also reported a slight decline in operating income as a percentage of net sales, from 12.6% to 10.8%. Despite these challenges, Ulta Beauty’s strategic acquisition of Space NK and new store contributions have bolstered its sales growth.

Ulta Beauty’s forward-looking outlook remains positive, with updated fiscal 2025 guidance projecting net sales of approximately $12.3 billion and a comparable sales increase of 4.4% to 4.7%. The company plans to continue expanding its store footprint and enhancing its digital and in-store experiences to capture market share and meet consumer demand.

Looking ahead, Ulta Beauty is poised to capitalize on the upcoming holiday season, with management expressing confidence in their strategic plans to drive excitement and deliver value to consumers, despite potential economic pressures. The company’s ongoing focus on innovation and customer engagement is expected to support sustained growth and profitability.

Docusign, Inc. is a leading provider of electronic signature solutions and contract lifecycle management, operating primarily in the technology sector. The company is recognized for its innovative Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform, which facilitates the creation, management, and execution of agreements for businesses worldwide.

In its third quarter fiscal 2026 earnings report, Docusign reported strong financial performance with significant growth in revenue and profitability. The company highlighted an 8% year-over-year increase in total revenue, reaching $818.4 million, with subscription revenue contributing $801.0 million. CEO Allan Thygesen attributed this success to robust customer investment in the IAM platform and improved operational efficiency.

Key financial metrics from the report include a 10% year-over-year increase in billings to $829.5 million and a notable rise in net income per diluted share to $0.40 from $0.30 in the previous year. The company also reported a substantial increase in free cash flow, amounting to $262.9 million, up from $210.7 million in the same period last year. Docusign’s strategic initiatives included expanding its AI ecosystem integrations and launching new capabilities within its IAM platform, further solidifying its market position.

Docusign’s forward-looking guidance anticipates continued growth, with expected revenue for the next quarter ranging between $825 million and $829 million, reflecting a 7% year-over-year increase. The company remains optimistic about its future prospects, driven by ongoing customer adoption of its IAM platform and strategic partnerships.

Overall, Docusign’s latest earnings report underscores its strong market position and strategic focus on innovation and customer engagement, setting a positive tone for future growth and development in the digital agreement space.

Looking ahead, Domo projects its fourth-quarter revenue to range between $78.0 million and $79.0 million, with a non-GAAP net loss per share expected to be between $0.01 and $0.05. data.title: Methode Electronics Reports Q2 Fiscal 2026 Results
data.description:
Methode Electronics ( (MEI) ) has released its Q2 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Methode Electronics presented to its investors.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Methode Electronics, Inc. is a global supplier of custom-engineered solutions for user interface, lighting, and power distribution applications, primarily serving the transportation, cloud computing, construction equipment, and consumer appliance sectors.

In its fiscal 2026 second quarter report, Methode Electronics announced net sales of $246.9 million, marking a 15.6% decrease year-over-year but a 2.6% increase from the previous quarter. The company reported a net loss of $9.9 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 7.1%, and reaffirmed its fiscal 2026 guidance for net sales between $900 million and $1 billion.

The company’s financial performance was impacted by a decline in sales volumes across all segments, notably in the Automotive segment, which saw a 24.1% drop in net sales due to the roll-off of legacy programs and lower sales volumes of sensor and user interface products. The Industrial segment experienced a slight decrease in net sales, while the Interface segment saw a significant decline in sales of touch panels for appliances. Despite these challenges, the Industrial segment reported an increase in income from operations due to higher gross profit and lower expenses.

Methode Electronics remains committed to its transformation journey, focusing on strategic operational enhancements to navigate market challenges, including a significant decline in annual net sales due to evolving demand in the electric vehicle market. The company continues to anticipate positive free cash flow for the year, reflecting its efforts to improve execution and resilience.

Looking ahead, Methode Electronics is focused on maintaining its fiscal 2026 guidance, driven by strategic improvements and operational resilience, while addressing challenges such as changes in EV demand and global economic instability.

For the third quarter, SentinelOne achieved a 23% year-over-year increase in total revenue, reaching $258.9 million, and a similar 23% rise in ARR, totaling $1,055.3 million. The company also reported a 20% growth in customers with ARR of $100,000 or more. Despite a slight decrease in gross margin, the operating margin improved significantly, with the GAAP operating margin rising from (42)% to (28)% and the non-GAAP operating margin turning positive at 7%. Additionally, the company reported a positive cash flow margin, reflecting improved financial health.

The company announced a leadership change with the departure of CFO Barbara Larson, who will be succeeded on an interim basis by Barry Padgett, the current Chief Growth Officer. This transition is expected to be smooth, given Padgett’s extensive experience in the industry.

Looking ahead, SentinelOne is optimistic about its growth prospects, with revenue guidance for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026 set at $271 million and full fiscal year 2026 revenue projected at $1,001 million. The company remains focused on maintaining its growth momentum while achieving sustainable profitability.

data.title: Stitch Fix Reports Revenue Growth Amid Client Decline
data.description:
Stitch Fix ( (SFIX) ) has released its Q1 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Stitch Fix presented to its investors.

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Stitch Fix, Inc. is a leading online personal styling service that combines human expertise with AI technology to provide personalized shopping experiences in the apparel and accessories sector. In its first quarter of fiscal 2026, Stitch Fix reported a 7.3% increase in net revenue year-over-year, reaching $342.1 million, despite a slight decline in active clients. The company achieved a net revenue per active client of $559, marking a 5.3% increase from the previous year, while gross profit rose by 3.1% to $149.3 million. However, the company faced a net loss of $6.4 million, translating to a diluted loss per share of $0.05. Stitch Fix also reported an adjusted EBITDA of $13.4 million and maintained a strong cash position with $244.2 million in cash and investments, and no debt. Looking forward, Stitch Fix anticipates net revenue between $335 million and $340 million for the second quarter of fiscal 2026, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of up to 3.8%. For the full fiscal year 2026, the company projects net revenue growth of up to 6.5% and expects to remain free cash flow positive.
